relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 58-year-old homeless man with hypertension, type II diabetes, and a history of prior venous thromboembolism presented with 1 week of progressive weakness and fatigue.
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Spontaneous retroperitoneal hematoma causing acute hemodynamic collapse with severe hemoglobin drop (12.3→6.8 g/dL), shock requiring ACLS and ICU admission. Matches pattern of vascular rupture causing hemodynamic collapse with bleeding.

relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: An 80-year-old woman with a history of rectal cancer treated in 2008 by liver metastasectomy followed by Hartmann's procedure with intraoperative
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Iatrogenic vascular injury with arterial bleeding from ureteroarterial fistula following pelvic surgery. Pulsatile arterial bleeding from ureteral orifice represents vascular access complication with active hemorrhage.

relevant_cases.diagnosis: Pseudoaneurysm of the lateral inferior genicular artery
relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 32-year-old man with no prior medical or surgical history presented to the emergency department with a 3-week history of a pulsatile mass
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Post-procedural (arthroscopy) iatrogenic arterial injury causing pseudoaneurysm. Direct procedural vascular complication requiring intervention.

relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 63-year-old man with a 3-day history of abdominal pain presented in shock. His medical history was notable for atrial fibrillation, stroke
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Spontaneous mesenteric hematoma causing hemodynamic collapse (BP 73/48 mmHg), anemia (Hb 9.0 g/dL), and shock while on anticoagulation. Bleeding causing vascular collapse with elevated venous pressures.

relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Post-surgical (Bentall procedure, aortoiliac bypass) vascular complication with bronchial artery pseudoaneurysm. Post-procedural vascular pseudoaneurysm requiring embolization.

relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Spontaneous ovarian vein rupture with retroperitoneal hematoma causing hemoglobin drop (9.5 g/dL). Spontaneous vascular rupture with bleeding complication.
